target:

patch_a_old_substring: but only because it is the globe’s answer expressed in dome notation. The formula that works is wholly WGS84
patch_a_new_substring: but only because it is the globe’s answer expressed in dome notation. At the only other geometrically-motivated evaluation point r = 20,015 km, Domain 4’s formula predicts g ≈ 9.7854 m/s², which violates Domain 4’s own published falsification criterion (“Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard”, raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71) by 4.8×. The formula that works is wholly WGS84
patch_b_new_substring:
fails by 170×.

The Mascon ↔ SAA r=0.92 correlation is not a dome-specific prediction. A dome defender might press Domain 19 (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 152) — “Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92” labelled ‘Sub-terrestrial structure’ — as a genuinely high empirical regularity between two global anomaly fields, and ask whether we engage it on its own terms. The r=0.92 is real, but it indicates shared causation, not independent prediction. Standard geophysics supplies the shared cause: East Antarctic crustal roots plus the uncompensated sub-lithospheric mantle plume (Pappa et al., 2019, JGR) produce both the crustal gravity low via isostatic imbalance and, through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure, contribute to the geometry of the SAA field minimum. The co-location of the two anomaly fields is a downstream consequence of common-cause geology, not a novel forecast. The dome’s contribution is to relabel the shared cause “Sub-terrestrial structure” without supplying any equation mapping dome geometry (disc radius, H(r), aetheric parameters) to either field — so r=0.92 is empirical regularity, not forecast.

The refraction cascade
word_count_delta: +1,622 characters (~250 words) added to detail_evidence across two insertions; no other fields touched. Field total: detail_evidence 7,155 → 8,777 chars.
holes_addressed: ISS-1385 / WIN-067.c8 H4 (moderate): the Domain 4 r=20,015 km falsification-violation — previously buried in detail_extra paragraph (iii) — is now surfaced in detail_evidence proper, inside the WGS84-tautology paragraph where it is most damning. One sentence makes visible to any reader that Domain 4 itself defines a falsification threshold (“Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard”, raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71) that the dome's own formula violates by 4.8× at its only other geometrically-motivated evaluation point. The curmudgeon c8 recommendation was to elevate “one sentence of the Domain 4 r=20,015 falsification-violation computation into detail_evidence proper” — the patch does exactly that, using the curmudgeon's suggested wording as the template.

ISS-1386 / WIN-067.c8 H5 (moderate): the Mascon ↔ SAA r=0.92 correlation is now engaged on its own terms in a dedicated paragraph rather than being implicitly dismissed as a “rebrand.” The rebuttal grants the kernel of truth (r=0.92 between two global anomaly fields is a real, high empirical regularity worth acknowledging) and then shows why it does not save the claim: high correlation between independently measured anomaly fields indicates shared causation, and standard geophysics supplies the shared cause (East Antarctic crustal roots plus uncompensated sub-lithospheric mantle plume — Pappa et al. 2019 JGR) which produces both the crustal gravity low via isostatic imbalance and, through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure, contributes to the geometry of the SAA field minimum. The dome's contribution reduces to a relabel (“Sub-terrestrial structure”) without an equation mapping dome geometry to either field — so r=0.92 is empirical regularity, not forecast.
new_evidence_added: Domain 4 falsification criterion text (“Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard”) quoted verbatim with raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71 citation — already verified in citation_check of WIN-067.c8 review.
Self-computed Domain 4 prediction at r=20,015 km: 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(−20015/8619)) = 9.7854 m/s², error vs WGS84 polar (9.8322 m/s²) = 0.476% = 4.76× the 0.1% falsification threshold.
Pappa et al. 2019 JGR DOI 10.1029/2019JB017997 (already cited elsewhere in detail_evidence; re-cited here for the shared-cause mechanism for the r=0.92 correlation).
Domain 19 raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 152 quotation “Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92” — verified in WIN-067.c8 citation_check.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: “You're still assuming monitor.py evaluates at r=20,015 km. It does not — it evaluates at r=0. Your new sentence invents an evaluation point the dome never intended.”
anticipated_objections.response: The new sentence is explicit: r=20,015 km is the only OTHER geometrically-motivated evaluation point (the rim of the disc, where the dome's own H(r) collapses to 837 km). The dome has published a formula with an exponential that decays over 8,619 km, but if the formula is never evaluated at any non-degenerate point, the exponential is inert and the formula reduces to the WGS84 polar anchor — the second-horn concession that detail_evidence already names. Either the formula has dome-specific content (in which case r=20,015 km is a legitimate test) or it does not (in which case detail_evidence's WGS84-tautology paragraph already stands). The falsification criterion is the dome author's own published test — he cannot escape it by refusing to evaluate his formula at a non-trivial radius.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: “r=0.92 is a high correlation and standard geophysics does not publish a specific model that predicts this exact coefficient. The dome flagged a relationship mainstream science has not quantified — give him credit.”
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged — the new paragraph explicitly grants “r=0.92 between two global anomaly fields is real” and calls it a “genuinely high empirical regularity.” The rebuttal is not that the correlation is wrong; it is that correlation is not prediction. Two fields shaped by the same underlying mantle/crustal structure will correlate as a downstream consequence of shared causation, which is precisely what Pappa 2019 + Ferraccioli 2011 + the PGIA literature describe. The dome has no equation mapping disc radius, H(r), aetheric pressure, or any dome-specific parameter to either field; its “Sub-terrestrial structure” label supplies no mechanism. Credit for observing the correlation is appropriate; credit for predicting it from dome geometry is not earned because no such prediction exists.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: “Your Patch A sentence conflicts with your own 170× headline in the closing line of the same paragraph. You said 1,430× earlier, then you say 170×, and now you add 4.8× — which is it?”
anticipated_objections.response: The three numbers measure three different things and are not in conflict: 1,430× is the apples-to-apples ratio of predicted-to-observed gravity drop at Antarctic latitude under linear g↔H(r) coupling; 170× is the conservative dimensionless lower bound from the whole-globe pole-equator fractional spread (also disclosed in-text as “170–1,430× depending on comparison” earlier in the paragraph chain); 4.8× is the specific violation of the dome's OWN falsification criterion (the dome author published “>0.1% vs WGS84 standard” as his threshold; 0.476% is 4.8× that). The new sentence does not replace the other two — it adds a third, self-executing falsification the dome author cannot escape by denying our framing because the threshold is his own. (Note: the 170×-only-no-qualifier sentence at the paragraph close is an inherited c7 carry-forward asymmetry scoped to a separate expansion — see curmudgeon WIN-067.c8 hole #1; this EXP targets only the two moderate holes flagged for EXP-221.)

kernel_of_truth: The Mascon ↔ SAA r=0.92 correlation is a real, high empirical regularity between two independently measured global anomaly fields — r=0.92 is not trivial and the dome author's identification of the relationship is worth acknowledging, not dismissed as a rebrand. The Antarctic gravity low is likewise a real geophysical anomaly that deserves explanation. Additionally, the dome's internal use of 8,619 km as a shared length scale across H(r) and λ_g is internally self-consistent — the number recurs across the model rather than being fitted per-feature. These are genuine strengths that the detail_evidence now engages with rather than glossing over, per the curmudgeon's c8 advocate-mode recommendation.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: A high correlation (r=0.92) between two geophysical anomaly fields indicates shared causation, not independent prediction. Standard geophysics supplies the shared cause — East Antarctic crustal roots plus the uncompensated sub-lithospheric mantle plume produce both the crustal gravity low (via isostatic imbalance) and contribute to the geometry of the SAA field minimum through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure. The correlation is a downstream consequence of common-cause geology, not a novel dome-specific forecast. Separately, the Domain 4 falsification-violation at r=20,015 km (Patch A) is self-executing: the dome author published >0.1% model error as his own failure threshold, and his own formula's output at the only non-degenerate geometrically-motivated evaluation point exceeds that threshold by 4.8×. The dome cannot rescue the claim without publishing an equation mapping dome geometry to either the Antarctic gravity low or the Mascon-SAA co-location — no such equation appears in V51.2.
